entities.name: SESAME
entities.description: An important indigenous oilseed crop (Sesamum indicum) whose growth and productivity are affected by abiotic stresses.

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Environmental factors that negatively impact the growth and productivity of crops like sesame.

entities.name: CLASS III PEROXIDASES
entities.description: Key stress-related enzymes found in plants, including the PRX gene family in sesame.

entities.name: SIPRXS
entities.description: The 45 non-redundant members of the class III peroxidase gene family identified in sesame.

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: A specific abiotic stress used to evaluate the expression and function of SiPRX genes in sesame.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of sesame accessions to maintain growth and productivity under drought stress.

entities.name: GENOME-WIDE ANALYSIS
entities.description: A computational approach used to identify the PRX gene family and their chromosomal distribution in sesame.

entities.name: MOTIF ANALYSIS
entities.description: A method used to identify highly conserved peroxidase domains in SiPRX proteins.

entities.name: PHENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of evaluating the physical characteristics of sesame accessions under drought and irrigated conditions.

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE REAL-TIME PCR
entities.description: A molecular technique used to measure the relative transcript abundance of specific SiPRX genes.

entities.name: EXPRESSION ANALYSIS
entities.description: The study of differential gene expression of SiPRX genes between drought-tolerant and drought-sensitive sesame accessions.

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT STRATEGIES
entities.description: The application of genomic and functional data to enhance drought resilience in sesame.
